channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Clint Hill, Secret Service agent who leaped onto JFK's car after the president was shot, dies at 93
item.description: Clint Hill received Secret Service awards and was promoted for his actions that day, but for decades blamed himself for Kennedy's death.
item.dc:creator: Tom Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge halts planned move of trans inmates to facilities for their biological sex
item.description: A federal judge has put a pause on the Trump/Bondi DOJ's plan to transfer transgender inmates to facilities corresponding to their biological sex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP governor in top energy state outlines how production 'held back' by Biden will 'open up' under Trump
item.description: Fox News Digital speaks with Wyoming Gov. Mark Gordon about his state's energy outlook at the National Governors Association Winter Meeting in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Northstar commuter trains from Minneapolis to Big Lake could be replaced with bus service
item.description: After more than 15 years, the days of Northstar commuter rail service between Minneapolis and Big Lake may be numbered. MnDOT and the Met Council are considering replacing Northstar commuter trains through the northwest Twin Cities metro area with buses.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: The few Republicans who still oppose Trump gather in search of a path to oppose him
item.description: Conservatives who filled a ballroom a few blocks from the White House have lamented that the United States is abandoning the fundamental ideals that forged a great nation.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kamala Harris takes veiled jabs at Trump, Elon Musk in 1st major speech since election defeat
item.description: Former Democratic presidential candidate, former Vice President Kamala Harris, indirectly derided President Donald Trump and Elon Musk during her NAACP speech.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE, EPA team up to claw back $67 million in 'environmental justice' grants
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency and the Department of Government Efficiency are cutting nearly $70 million in Biden era environmental grants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US-Ukraine rift breaks out at United Nations on 3rd anniversary of the war
item.description: The U.S. is calling on members of the United Nations General Assembly to nix a proposed resolution from Ukraine demanding Russian troops withdrawal from the country, in favor of their own resolution that urges lasting peace but does not compel Russia to leave the war-torn region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump meets France's Macron at White House, says 'lot of progress' made toward ending war in Ukraine
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is at the White House meeting with President Donald Trump as the U.S. is seeking an end of the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In window before special election, House Republicans push for votes on their agenda
item.description: With a slim majority, Republicans in the Minnesota House are pushing forward bills that are likely not to pass a floor vote. They hope it will still send a political message.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: US pressures Ukraine to nix its UN resolution demanding Russian forces withdraw
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.S. has pressured Ukraine to withdraw its European-backed U.N. resolution demanding an immediate withdrawal of Russian forces from Ukraine in favor of an American proposal that does not mention Moscow’s invasion, a U.S. official and a European diplomat said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Proposed bill may criminalize landlords, property management for unpaid utilities
item.description: Jackson says he was inspired to write the bill after dilapidated apartments, like the Jolie and Woodlawn Terrace Apartments, forced law-abiding tenants out of their homes.
item.dc:creator: Myriam Samake, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic governors balance whether to fight or pacify after Trump threatens one of their own
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's real-time confrontation with Maine's governor over transgender athletes captured the conundrum many Democratic governors are facing in the Republican's second term. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy offers to resign in exchange for peace or Ukraine’s NATO membership
item.description: “If it’s about peace in Ukraine, and you really want me to leave my position – I am ready to do that," Zelenskyy told reporters at a press conference in Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers sue Elon Musk over email ultimatum
item.description: The lawsuit seeks to block mass layoffs, including any connected to an email distributed by the Office of Personnel Management on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: States threaten fines and jail time for local officials who resist Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Republican state lawmakers seeking to aid President Donald Trump’s crackdown on illegal immigration are threatening local officials who resist with lawsuits, fines and even potential jail time.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump revels in mass federal firings and jeers at Biden before adoring conservative crowd
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Saturday that “nobody's ever seen anything" like his administration's sweeping effort to fire thousands of federal employees and shrink the size of government, congratulating himself for “dominating” Washington and sending bureaucrats “packing.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: National Park Service rangers laid off as DOGE makes cuts
item.description: National Park Service ranger Mikayla Moors was one of thousands of government employees laid off by the DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump‘s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A U.S. district judge granted a preliminary injunction blocking the administration from terminating or changing federal contracts they consider equity-related.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Elon Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Republicans approve budget framework, pushing past Democratic objections after all-night vote
item.description: The early Friday vote was a key step toward providing money the Trump administration says it needs funds for mass deportations and security measures that top the Republican agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Justice Department broadens Jan. 6 pardons to cover gun, drug-related charges
item.description: In a new development, the Department of Justice said Trump's pardon of Jan. 6 defendants should apply more broadly and include separate gun charges, as well.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling controversial LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida cabinet assumes new role, sparking criticism over privacy and power
item.description: After a month-long feud over who would be the state's chief immigration enforcement officer, state lawmakers passed a bill package that grants not one, but four elected officials with that task.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republican introduces bill to make Trump's birthday a federal holiday
item.description: The Trump’s Birthday and Flag Day Holiday Establishment Act proposes the recognition of both Trump’s birthday and Flag Day, celebrated on June 14, as federal public holidays.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a January fatal midair collision at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5
